executive_summary: Cyber threat intelligence digest for 2026-03-10. 120 live items from 16 sources. 22 CRITICAL and 46 HIGH severity events in the last 36 hours. Top threats: CISA: Recently patched Ivanti EPM flaw now actively exploited; APT28 hackers deploy customized variant of Covenant open-source tool; ShinyHunters claims ongoing Salesforce Aura data theft attacks. All items are from today only — no recycled or old news.

Top threats: CISA: Recently patched Ivanti EPM flaw now actively exploited; APT28 hackers deploy customized variant of Covenant open-source tool; ShinyHunters claims ongoing Salesforce Aura data theft attacks. All items are from today only — no recycled or old news.

items.summary: Salesforce is warning customers that hackers are targeting websites with misconfigured Experience Cloud platforms that give guest users access to more data than intended. However, the ShinyHunters extortion gang claims to be actively exploiting a new bug to steal data from instances. [...]

items.title: The Zero-Day Scramble is Avoidable: A Guide to Attack Surface Reduction
items.summary:
You can't control when the next critical vulnerability drops. You can control how much of your environment is exposed when it does. The problem is that most teams have more internet-facing exposure than they realise. Intruder's Head of Security digs into why this happens and how teams can manage it deliberately.

items.title: SurxRAT Android Malware Uses LLMs for Phishing and Data Theft
items.summary: A new Android Remote Access Trojan (RAT) named SurxRAT, which is being sold as a commercial malware platform through a Telegram-based malware‑as‑a‑service (MaaS) ecosystem. The malware, marketed under the SURXRAT V5 branding, enables cybercriminals to create customized Android malware builds capable of surveillance, credential theft, remote device control, and ransomware-style device locking. items.title: APT28 hackers deploy customized variant of Covenant open-source tool
items.summary: The Russian state-sponsored APT28 threat group is using a custom variant of the open-source Covenant post-exploitation framework for long-term espionage operations. [...]

items.title: APT28 Uses BEARDSHELL and COVENANT Malware to Spy on Ukrainian Military
items.summary:
The Russian state-sponsored hacking group tracked as APT28 has been observed using a pair of implants dubbed BEARDSHELL and COVENANT to facilitate long‑term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel.
The two malware families have been put to use since April 2024, ESET said in a new report shared with The Hacker News.
APT28, also tracked as Blue Athena, BlueDelta, Fancy Bear, Fighting Ursa,

items.title: Russian military hackers revive advanced malware to spy on Ukraine, researchers say
items.summary: Russian state hacker group APT28 has revived a sophisticated cyber-espionage toolkit to spy on Ukrainian targets, including military personnel, according to a report published Tuesday by cybersecurity firm ESET.

items.title: Hackers may have breached FBI wiretap network via supply chain
items.summary: A suspected breach of the FBI’s wiretap network has investigators asking whether a nation-state was involved.

items.title: APT28 conducts long-term espionage on Ukrainian forces using custom malware
items.summary: APT28 used BEARDSHELL and COVENANT malware to spy on Ukrainian military personnel, enabling long-term surveillance since April 2024. The Russia-linked group APT28 (aka UAC-0001, aka Fancy Bear, Pawn Storm, Sofacy Group, Sednit, BlueDelta, and STRONTIUM) has used BEARDSHELL and COVENANT malware to conduct long-term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel. According to ESET, the campaign began

items.title: CVE-2026-3799 [CVSS 8.8] — A flaw has been found in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This impacts the function formS
items.summary: A flaw has been found in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This impacts the function formSetCfm of the file /goform/setcfm. This manipulation of the argument funcpara1 causes stack-based buffer overflow. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been published and may be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-3801 [CVSS 8.8] — A vulnerability was found in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this vulnerabil
items.summary: A vulnerability was found in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this vulnerability is the function formSetAutoPing of the file /goform/setAutoPing. Performing a manipulation of the argument ping1/ping2 results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-3802 [CVSS 8.8] — A vulnerability was determined in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this issue
items.summary: A vulnerability was determined in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this issue is the function formexeCommand of the file /goform/exeCommand. Executing a manipulation of the argument cmdinput can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

items.title: CVE-2026-30896 [CVSS 7.8] — The installer for Qsee Client versions 1.0.1 and prior insecurely load Dynamic L
items.summary: The installer for Qsee Client versions 1.0.1 and prior insecurely load Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs). When a user is directed to place some malicious DLL to the same directory and execute the affected installer, then arbitrary code may be executed with the administrative privilege.

items.title: CVE-2026-3803 [CVSS 8.8] — A vulnerability was identified in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This affects the funct
items.summary: A vulnerability was identified in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This affects the function formWifiMacFilterGet of the file /goform/WifiMacFilterGet. The manipulation of the argument index leads to stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-3804 [CVSS 8.8] — A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This vulnerabilit
items.summary: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). This vulnerability affects the function formWifiMacFilterSet of the file /goform/WifiMacFilterSet. The manipulation of the argument index results in stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

items.summary:
Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls.

items.title: BeatBanker: A dual‑mode Android Trojan
items.summary: Kaspersky researchers identified a new Android Trojan dubbed BeatBanker targeting Brazil, posing as government apps and Google Play Store, and capable of both crypto mining and stealing banking data.

items.title: Fake Claude Code install pages hit Windows and Mac users with infostealers
items.summary: Researchers uncovered fake Claude Code install pages spreading infostealers that steal passwords and browser sessions.

items.title: Quiz sites trick users into enabling unwanted browser notifications
items.summary: The quiz is just bait. The real goal is to win permission to send browser notifications that can later be used for ads, scams, or shady promotions.

items.title: A week in security (March 2 – March 8)
items.summary: A list of topics we covered in the week of March 2 to March 8 of 2026

items.title: New KadNap botnet hijacks ASUS routers to fuel cybercrime proxy network
items.summary: A newly discovered botnet malware called KadNap is targeting ASUS routers and other edge networking devices to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]

items.title: The New Turing Test: How Threats Use Geometry to Prove 'Humanness'
items.summary: Malware is evolving to evade sandboxes by pretending to be a real human behind the keyboard. The Picus Red Report 2026 shows 80% of top attacker techniques now focus on evasion and persistence, including geometry-based cursor tests and CPU timing checks. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Teams phishing targets employees with A0Backdoor malware
items.summary: Hackers contacted employees at financial and healthcare organizations over Microsoft Teams to trick them into granting remote access through Quick Assist and deploy a new piece of malware called A0Backdoor. [...]

items.title: Google: Cloud attacks exploit flaws more than weak credentials
items.summary: Hackers are increasingly exploiting newly disclosed vulnerabilities in third-party software to gain initial access to cloud environments, with the window for attacks shrinking from weeks to just days. [...]

items.title: Ericsson US discloses data breach after service provider hack
items.summary: Ericsson Inc., the U.S. subsidiary of Swedish networking and telecommunications giant Ericsson, says attackers have stolen data belonging to over 15,000 employees and customers after hacking one of its service providers. [...]

items.title: FBI warns of phishing attacks impersonating US city, county officials
items.summary: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warns that criminals are impersonating U.S. officials in phishing attacks targeting businesses and individuals who request city and county planning and zoning permits. [...]

items.title: Why Password Audits Miss the Accounts Attackers Actually Want
items.summary: Password audits often focus on complexity rules but miss the accounts attackers actually target. Specops Software explains how breached passwords, orphaned users, and service accounts can leave organizations exposed. [...]

items.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries

The shortcomings have been collectively named LeakyLooker by Tenable. There is no evidence that the vulnerabilities were exploited in

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer just a tool we talk to; it is a tool that does things for us. These are called AI Agents. They can send emails, move data, and even manage software on their own.
But there is a problem. While these agents make work faster, they also open a new "back door" for hackers.

raw_items.title: Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
raw_items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts.
The package, named "@openclaw-ai/openclawai," was uploaded to the registry by a user named "openclaw-ai" on March 3, 2026. It has been downloaded 178 times to date. The library is still available for

raw_items.summary:

raw_items.title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls
raw_items.summary:
Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls.
The post Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls appeared first on Unit 42.

raw_items.title: 9th March – Threat Intelligence Report
raw_items.summary:
For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 9th March, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES AkzoNobel, a Netherlands-based global paint manufacturer, has confirmed a cyberattack affecting one of its United States sites. The company said the intrusion was contained, while the Anubis ransomware group claimed it stole […]

raw_items.title: BeatBanker: A dual‑mode Android Trojan
raw_items.summary: Kaspersky researchers identified a new Android Trojan dubbed BeatBanker targeting Brazil, posing as government apps and Google Play Store, and capable of both crypto mining and stealing banking data.

raw_items.title: Signal and WhatsApp accounts targeted in phishing campaign
raw_items.summary: Dutch intelligence warns that attackers are hijacking Signal and WhatsApp accounts by tricking users into sharing verification codes or linking a malicious device.

raw_items.title: Hackers may have breached FBI wiretap network via supply chain
raw_items.summary: A suspected breach of the FBI’s wiretap network has investigators asking whether a nation-state was involved.

raw_items.title: Fake Claude Code install pages hit Windows and Mac users with infostealers
raw_items.summary: Researchers uncovered fake Claude Code install pages spreading infostealers that steal passwords and browser sessions.

raw_items.title: Quiz sites trick users into enabling unwanted browser notifications
raw_items.summary: The quiz is just bait. The real goal is to win permission to send browser notifications that can later be used for ads, scams, or shady promotions.

APT28 conducts long-term espionage on Ukrainian forces using custom malware
raw_items.summary: APT28 used BEARDSHELL and COVENANT malware to spy on Ukrainian military personnel, enabling long-term surveillance since April 2024. The Russia-linked group APT28 (aka UAC-0001, aka Fancy Bear, Pawn Storm, Sofacy Group, Sednit, BlueDelta, and STRONTIUM) has used BEARDSHELL and COVENANT malware to conduct long-term surveillance of Ukrainian military personnel. raw_items.summary: Attackers are mass-scanning Salesforce Experience Cloud sites using a modified AuraInspector tool to exploit misconfigurations and access sensitive data. Salesforce CSOC warns that threat actors are mass-scanning publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites using a modified version of the AuraInspector tool. raw_items.title: Law enforcement disrupted Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform
raw_items.summary: Authorities disrupted the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform used to send millions of phishing emails to over 500,000 orgs worldwide. The joint effort, led by Microsoft, Europol, and industry partners, aimed to target the infrastructure of Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform responsible for tens of millions of fraudulent emails reaching over 500,000 organizations each month worldwide.

Maintaining a structured patch management cycle aligned with this monthly schedule remains a foundational practice for enterprise SAP security. raw_items.title: OpenClaw Advisory Surge Highlights Blind Spot Between GitHub and CVE Vulnerability Tracking
raw_items.summary: OpenClaw’s rapid rise has accidentally exposed how far GitHub’s advisory ecosystem has drifted from traditional CVE‑centric vulnerability tracking. Within roughly three weeks, the project published more than 200 GitHub Security Advisories (GHSA), and its advisory page now lists around 255 disclosures covering command execution controls, authorization checks, allowlist logic, and plugin boundaries.

raw_items.title: Gogs Flaw Could Let Attackers Quietly Overwrite Large File Storage Data
raw_items.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been identified in Gogs, a widely used open-source self-hosted Git service. / Tracked as CVE-2026-25921, this flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to silently overwrite Git Large File Storage (LFS) objects across any repository. By exploiting a lack of content verification, threat actors can conduct stealthy software supply-chain attacks, replacing legitimate

raw_items.title: Cloudflare Pingora Flaws Enable Request Smuggling and Cache Poisoning Attacks
raw_items.summary: In a recent security advisory, Cloudflare disclosed multiple HTTP request smuggling and cache poisoning vulnerabilities in its open-source Pingora framework. Tracked under the identifiers CVE-2026-2833, CVE-2026-2835, and CVE-2026-2836, these flaws specifically impact standalone Pingora deployments that are exposed directly to the internet as ingress proxies. raw_items.summary: A vulnerability has been found in SourceCodester/janobe Resort Reservation System 1.0. Affected is the function doInsert of the file /controller.php?action=add. raw_items.summary: A vulnerability was found in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this vulnerability is the function formSetAutoPing of the file /goform/setAutoPing. Performing a manipulation of the argument ping1/ping2 results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

raw_items.title: CVE-2026-3822 [CVSS 6.5] — Taipower APP developed by Taipower has an Improper Certificate Validation vulner
raw_items.summary: Taipower APP developed by Taipower has an Improper Certificate Validation vulnerability. When establishing an HTTPS connection with the server, the application fails to verify the server-side TLS/SSL certificate. This flaw allows an unauthenticated remote attackers to exploit the vulnerability to perform a Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attack to read and tamper with network packets.

raw_items.summary: A vulnerability was determined in Tenda i3 1.0.0.6(2204). Affected by this issue is the function formexeCommand of the file /goform/exeCommand. Executing a manipulation of the argument cmdinput can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

raw_items.title: CVE-2026-30896 [CVSS 7.8] — The installer for Qsee Client versions 1.0.1 and prior insecurely load Dynamic L
raw_items.summary: The installer for Qsee Client versions 1.0.1 and prior insecurely load Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs). When a user is directed to place some malicious DLL to the same directory and execute the affected installer, then arbitrary code may be executed with the administrative privilege.
